title: Trace Source into the Accessibility Tree
summary: Build and verify accessibility tree in survey control announcement investigation through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain accessibility tree from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply accessibility tree without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

Survey control announcement investigation case 1: read the screenshot aloud because pixels are the accessibility tree.

options.text: Survey control announcement investigation case 4: keep the survey control announcement investigation defect because the happy path worked once.

Survey control announcement investigation case 4: replace the complete artifact before isolating the failed relationship.

steps.title: 05 · Read the survey control announcement investigation case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves accessibility tree when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey control announcement investigation.
steps.why: Trace Source into the Accessibility Tree makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in survey control announcement investigation.

content.text: Browsers derive an accessibility tree from DOM semantics, accessible names, roles, states, properties, and rendered availability. Assistive technologies consume that platform representation, not a screenshot. CSS, hidden states, labels, native elements, and ARIA can change what is exposed. Inspecting the tree explains what a control is, what it is called, and its current state. Apply it to survey control announcement investigation.
